scope: V20-GAP-04 External Agent Host / AionUi-like contract
V20-GAP-05 External Chat Client / Cherry-like Agent API
V20-GAP-07 browser-use-like browser tool provider contract
V20-GAP-08 Bridge / Remote / IDE / CI clean-room facade
V20-GAP-09 UI/TUI work-state and operator dashboard
V20-GAP-10 Cost / evidence / recovery visible state

rows.releaseRisk: External host must remain facade only; task execution must enter DSXU Query Loop, Tool Gate, Context and Evidence.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run one external-host style task through existing SDK/bridge/remote IO surfaces and capture permission, message, final evidence.

rows.capability: External Chat Client / Cherry-like Agent API
rows.owner: Query Loop API + Model Router boundary
rows.releaseRisk: Chat clients can be API callers only; they must not receive a second tool runner or provider runtime.
rows.nextAcceptance: Define and run one local-agent API smoke that proves tool calls and model selection stay in DSXU owners.

rows.releaseRisk: Terminal host is transport/UI only; command execution remains under Tool Gate and permission evidence.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run terminal-host scenario with failure recovery and permission-visible command trace.

rows.releaseRisk: Browser provider is a controlled tool provider, not an imported browser-use runtime.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run one browser operation with manifest, permission decision, tool trace, screenshot/DOM/console and final report.

rows.capability: Bridge / Remote / IDE / CI clean-room facade
rows.releaseRisk: Bridge must not directly execute tools or own query runtime; permission responses remain DSXU control-plane projections.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run bridge lifecycle acceptance covering SDK message, permission request/response, reconnect/failure state and final evidence.

rows.releaseRisk: UI/TUI may project work-state but must not hide a runner or make blocked work look done.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run operator-state acceptance across progress, permission, MCP, agent, cost, recovery and evidence links.

rows.releaseRisk: Cost/recovery evidence cannot stay test-only; it must appear in real final reports and visible state.
rows.nextAcceptance: Run real coding recovery task proving route reason, cost, cache, recovery and final evidence are all visible.

focusedVerification.scope: Bridge/Remote lifecycle, browser proof, final-report cost evidence owner smoke only

rule: All reviewed gaps have named owner coverage. None are final PASS until real acceptance evidence exists. Do not add external-host, chat-client, terminal, browser, bridge, UI, cost or recovery as a second runtime.
